message:
Add exit code preservation tests (bd-1yj8)

Implements true E2E tests for exit code propagation:
- Local vs remote exit code comparison verification
- Cargo build/test success (exit 0)
- Build errors via cargo (exit 101)
- Test failures (exit 101)
- Direct rustc success/error (exit 0/1)
- gcc via make success/error (exit 0/2)
- make missing target error (exit 2)
- Signal exit codes: SIGTERM (143), SIGKILL (137)
- Arbitrary exit code propagation (0, 1, 2, 42, 100, 127, 255)

New fixtures:
- broken_c: C compilation errors
- broken_make: Makefile with missing prerequisite
- broken_rustc: Direct rustc files (valid.rs, broken.rs)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
 {"id":"bd-105u","title":"Implement BuildErrorDisplay for compilation failures","description":"Create BuildErrorDisplay in rch-common/src/ui/errors/build.rs for build-related errors:
- Compilation errors with compiler output passthrough
- Build timeout with resource usage at timeout
- Missing dependencies with installation hints
- Workspace sync failures with file list
- Artifact retrieval failures with path details

Technical requirements:
- Preserve and enhance rustc/cargo error formatting (don't override)
- Add RCH context header before compiler output
- Show build command that was executed
- Include worker resource state (disk, memory) for resource errors
- Differentiate between local and remote failures
- Suggest cache clear for bizarre build failures

Example for timeout:
╔═[ERROR]═══════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║ ✗ RCH-E301: Build Timeout                             ║
╠═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║ Compilation exceeded timeout on worker 'build1'       ║
║                                                        ║
║ Command: cargo build --release                        ║
║ Duration: 300s (timeout: 300s)                        ║
║ Last output: Compiling serde_derive v1.0.152          ║
║                                                        ║
║ Worker state at timeout:                              ║
║   CPU: 98% │ Memory: 14.2/16 GB │ Load: 8.5           ║
║                                                        ║
║ Suggestions:                                           ║
║   1. Increase timeout: rch compile --timeout 600      ║
║   2. Check worker load: rch workers list              ║
║   3. Try different worker: rch compile --worker build2│
║   4. Build incrementally: rch compile (without clean) ║
╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:09:09.220512555Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T21:13:13.557938134Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-105u","depends_on_id":"bd-3r1e","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:09:09.249116391Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-105u","depends_on_id":"bd-m065","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:13:13.557890294Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-10g8","title":"Test: cargo test Remote Execution & Exit Codes","description":"## Purpose
Test that `cargo test` commands are correctly offloaded to real workers with proper exit code handling.

## MANDATORY Logging
This test MUST use TestLogger for all phases:

```json
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_cargo_test_pass","phase":"execute","msg":"Running cargo test","data":{"cmd":"cargo test","filter":"none","worker":"css"}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_cargo_test_pass","phase":"verify","msg":"Exit code check","data":{"expected":0,"actual":0,"match":true,"tests_run":5,"tests_passed":5}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_cargo_test_fail","phase":"verify","msg":"Exit code check","data":{"expected":101,"actual":101,"match":true,"tests_run":5,"tests_failed":1}}
```

## Test Cases

### Basic Test Execution
1. All tests pass:
   - Command: `cargo test`
   - Expected: exit code 0
   - Verify: test output shows passes
   - Log: exit code, tests run, tests passed

2. Some tests fail:
   - Command: `cargo test` (on project with failing tests)
   - Expected: exit code 101
   - Verify: failure output preserved
   - Log: exit code, tests failed, failure output snippet

3. Compilation error:
   - Command: `cargo test` (on broken project)
   - Expected: exit code 1
   - Verify: compiler error in output
   - Log: exit code, error type

### Test Filtering
4. Filter by name:
   - Command: `cargo test test_name_pattern`
   - Expected: only matching tests run
   - Log: filter pattern, tests matched

5. Run ignored tests:
   - Command: `cargo test -- --ignored`
   - Expected: ignored tests execute
   - Log: ignored tests count

6. Show output:
   - Command: `cargo test -- --nocapture`
   - Expected: stdout visible in output
   - Log: output capture mode

### Test Targets
7. Lib tests only:
   - Command: `cargo test --lib`
   - Expected: only library tests
   - Log: target filter

8. Doc tests:
   - Command: `cargo test --doc`
   - Expected: documentation tests run
   - Log: doc test count

### Special Cases
9. Test thread control:
   - Command: `RUST_TEST_THREADS=4 cargo test`
   - Expected: respects thread limit
   - Log: thread config

10. Workspace tests:
    - Command: `cargo test --workspace`
    - Expected: all packages tested
    - Log: packages tested

## Exit Code Semantics
- 0 = all tests passed (log: success)
- 1 = compilation/build error (log: build_error)
- 101 = tests ran but some failed (log: tests_failed)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All exit codes correctly propagated
- [ ] Test output preserved (including colors)
- [ ] Filter flags work correctly
- [ ] Environment variables respected
- [ ] ALL phases logged with structured JSON","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:23:27.577283284Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T07:35:35.578726502Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-10g8","depends_on_id":"bd-12hi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:23:39.099971670Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-10g8","depends_on_id":"bd-17tg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:23:40.107623415Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

### Test Filtering

 {"id":"bd-12h6","title":"E2E: Daemon Stability Test with rich_rust","description":"## Test: rchd Daemon Stability

Verify that rchd daemon functions correctly with rich_rust integration. The daemon is a long-running service that must remain stable.

### Test Scenarios

#### 1. Startup and Shutdown
```bash
# Clean startup
rchd start &
sleep 2
rchd stop

# Verify clean shutdown
ps aux | grep rchd | grep -v grep && fail "Daemon still running"
```

#### 2. Rich Output Doesn't Block
```bash
# Daemon should not block on rich output if stderr is not a TTY
rchd start > /dev/null 2>&1 &
DAEMON_PID=$!

sleep 2
kill -0 $DAEMON_PID || fail "Daemon died unexpectedly"

# Perform operations
rch status || true
rch workers list || true

# Daemon should still be healthy
kill -0 $DAEMON_PID || fail "Daemon crashed during operations"

rchd stop
```

#### 3. High Volume Job Processing
```bash
# Process 100 jobs rapidly
for i in $(seq 1 100); do
    echo '{"tool":"Bash","input":{"command":"echo test"}}' | rch hook --stdin &
done
wait

# Daemon should not OOM or crash
rch status || fail "Daemon unhealthy after load test"
```

#### 4. Memory Stability (No Leaks)
```bash
# Monitor memory over time
INITIAL_MEM=$(ps -o rss= -p $DAEMON_PID)

# Process 1000 jobs
for i in $(seq 1 1000); do
    echo '{"tool":"Bash","input":{"command":"echo test"}}' | rch hook --stdin >/dev/null 2>&1
done

FINAL_MEM=$(ps -o rss= -p $DAEMON_PID)
GROWTH=$((FINAL_MEM - INITIAL_MEM))

# Allow some growth but not unbounded
if (( GROWTH > 50000 )); then  # 50MB
    fail "Memory growth too high: ${GROWTH}KB"
fi
```

### Implementation

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# scripts/test_daemon_stability.sh
set -euo pipefail

source scripts/test_lib.sh
init_test_log "daemon_stability"

RCHD="./target/release/rchd"
RCH="./target/release/rch"

cleanup() {
    "$RCH" daemon stop 2>/dev/null || true
    pkill -f rchd 2>/dev/null || true
}
trap cleanup EXIT

log_json "setup" "Building binaries"
cargo build -p rch -p rchd --features rich-ui --release

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 1: Startup/Shutdown Cycle
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log_json "test" "Startup/shutdown cycle"

for i in 1 2 3; do
    "$RCH" daemon start >/dev/null 2>&1
    sleep 1
    
    if ! "$RCH" status >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        fail "Daemon not responding on cycle $i"
    fi
    
    "$RCH" daemon stop >/dev/null 2>&1
    sleep 1
done

log_json "verify" "Startup/shutdown cycles passed" '{"cycles":3}'

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 2: Background Operation (no TTY)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log_json "test" "Background operation without TTY"

# Start daemon with all output redirected
"$RCH" daemon start > /tmp/daemon_stdout.txt 2> /tmp/daemon_stderr.txt &
sleep 2

if ! "$RCH" status >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    fail "Daemon failed in background mode"
fi

# Rich output should go to stderr, not block startup
if [[ -s /tmp/daemon_stderr.txt ]]; then
    log_json "info" "Daemon stderr output" "{\"bytes\":$(wc -c < /tmp/daemon_stderr.txt)}"
fi

"$RCH" daemon stop

log_json "verify" "Background operation passed" '{"passed":true}'

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 3: Load Test
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log_json "test" "Load test (100 concurrent hook invocations)"

"$RCH" daemon start
sleep 2

START=$(date +%s%N)
for i in $(seq 1 100); do
    echo '{"tool":"Bash","input":{"command":"echo test"}}' | "$RCH" hook --stdin >/dev/null 2>&1 &
done
wait
END=$(date +%s%N)

DURATION_MS=$(( (END - START) / 1000000 ))
log_json "timing" "Load test duration" "{\"jobs\":100,\"duration_ms\":$DURATION_MS}"

if ! "$RCH" status >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    fail "Daemon unhealthy after load test"
fi

"$RCH" daemon stop

log_json "verify" "Load test passed" "{\"jobs\":100,\"duration_ms\":$DURATION_MS}"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SUMMARY
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log_json "summary" "All daemon stability tests passed"
echo ""
echo "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
echo "ALL DAEMON STABILITY TESTS PASSED"
echo "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
```

### Files

- CREATE: scripts/test_daemon_stability.sh
- CREATE: rchd/tests/stability.rs","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:42:53.940724107Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T21:42:53.959018319Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-12h6","depends_on_id":"bd-1z6p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:42:53.958909734Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-12hi","title":"True E2E Cargo Compilation Tests (build/test/check/clippy)","description":"# Feature: True E2E Cargo Compilation Tests

## Purpose

Validate that all Cargo-based compilation kinds work correctly when executed on real remote workers. This is the **most critical** test category because Rust/Cargo is the primary use case for RCH.

## MANDATORY Logging Requirements

All tests in this feature MUST use the structured JSON logging infrastructure from bd-3saj:

### Log Events Per Test

```json
// Test start
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_cargo_build_simple","phase":"setup","msg":"Starting Cargo build test","data":{"fixture":"simple_binary","worker":"css"}}

// Sync phase
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_cargo_build_simple","phase":"sync","msg":"Project synced","data":{"files":42,"bytes":123456,"duration_ms":234}}

// Local execution
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_cargo_build_simple","phase":"execute_local","msg":"Running local cargo build","data":{"cmd":"cargo build","exit_code":0,"duration_ms":5432}}

// Remote execution
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_cargo_build_simple","phase":"execute_remote","msg":"Running remote cargo build","data":{"cmd":"cargo build","exit_code":0,"worker":"css","duration_ms":3210}}

// Comparison
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_cargo_build_simple","phase":"validate","msg":"Outputs match","data":{"stdout_match":true,"stderr_match":true,"exit_code_match":true}}

// Artifact check
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_cargo_build_simple","phase":"validate","msg":"Artifact verified","data":{"path":"target/debug/mybin","size":1234567,"exists":true}}

// Test end
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_cargo_build_simple","phase":"teardown","msg":"Test completed","data":{"result":"pass","total_duration_ms":9876}}
```

### Failure Logging

On any assertion failure, log detailed diff information:

```json
{"ts":"...","level":"ERROR","test":"test_cargo_build_simple","phase":"validate","msg":"Output mismatch","data":{"field":"stdout","local_lines":42,"remote_lines":43,"diff":"<unified diff>"}}
```

## CompilationKind Coverage

The following `CompilationKind` variants must have true e2e tests:

| Kind | Command Example | Priority | Notes |
|------|-----------------|----------|-------|
| CargoBuild | `cargo build --release` | P0 | Core functionality |
| CargoTest | `cargo test` | P0 | Tests must report correctly |
| CargoCheck | `cargo check` | P1 | Fast validation |
| CargoClippy | `cargo clippy` | P1 | Lints must match local |
| CargoBench | `cargo bench` | P2 | Benchmark output |
| CargoNextest | `cargo nextest run` | P2 | Alternative test runner |
| CargoDoc | `cargo doc` | P3 | Documentation generation |

## Test Scenarios Per Kind

### CargoBuild Tests
1. **Simple build** - Single binary crate
2. **Workspace build** - Multi-crate workspace
3. **Release build** - `--release` flag honored
4. **Feature flags** - `--features` passed correctly
5. **Target directory** - Artifacts in correct location
6. **Incremental rebuild** - Only changed files recompiled

### CargoTest Tests
1. **All tests pass** - Exit code 0, output matches
2. **Some tests fail** - Exit code 101, failure output captured
3. **Filtered tests** - `cargo test my_test` runs subset
4. **Test threads** - `--test-threads=1` honored
5. **Nocapture** - `-- --nocapture` shows println output
6. **Doc tests** - `--doc` flag runs doc tests

### CargoCheck Tests
1. **Clean check** - No warnings
2. **With warnings** - Warnings captured in output
3. **With errors** - Compilation errors reported correctly

### CargoClippy Tests
1. **No warnings** - Clean clippy run
2. **With lints** - Lint warnings match local output
3. **Deny warnings** - `-D warnings` causes failure correctly

## Test Project Templates

Create minimal test projects in `tests/true_e2e/fixtures/`:

```
fixtures/
├── simple_binary/      # Single main.rs binary
├── simple_library/     # Single lib.rs library  
├── with_tests/         # Has #[test] functions
├── with_failures/      # Has tests that fail
├── workspace/          # Multi-crate workspace
└── with_build_rs/      # Custom build script
```

## Output Validation

For each test, validate AND LOG:
1. **Exit code** matches local execution (log both values)
2. **Compiled artifacts** exist and are valid (log path, size, hash)
3. **Stdout/stderr** match with path normalization (log diff on mismatch)
4. **Timing** is within reasonable bounds (log local vs remote ms)

## Special Considerations

### Test Isolation
Each test should use a unique temporary copy of fixtures to prevent state pollution between tests. Log fixture copy path.

### Toolchain Compatibility
Tests should detect if worker has compatible Rust version and skip if not. Log skip reason as structured JSON.

### Workspace Member Handling
Ensure `-p package_name` works correctly for workspace builds.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] CargoBuild: simple, workspace, release, features tests pass
- [ ] CargoTest: pass, fail, filtered, nocapture tests pass
- [ ] CargoCheck: clean, warnings, errors tests pass
- [ ] CargoClippy: clean, with-lints tests pass
- [ ] All tests validate output matches local execution
- [ ] Tests skip gracefully on toolchain mismatch
- [ ] ALL tests emit structured JSON logs per phase
- [ ] Failure logs include detailed diff information","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:10:50.308246221Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T18:37:17.677279444Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-12hi","depends_on_id":"bd-1cwg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:10:50.336723969Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-12hi","depends_on_id":"bd-3saj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:10:56.510554144Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-148y","title":"Unit Tests for CLI Renderables (StatusTable, WorkerTable, etc.)","description":"## Unit Tests for Phase 2 CLI Renderables

Comprehensive unit tests for each CLI command renderable component.

### Components to Test

1. **StatusTable** (bd-bv6s)
2. **WorkerTable** (bd-3cny)
3. **ProbeResult** (bd-mlps)
4. **BenchmarkTable** (bd-3byb)
5. **ConfigDisplay** (bd-3q6w)

### Test Strategy

Each component needs:
- Rendering at different widths (40, 80, 120 cols)
- Rich vs Plain output modes
- Unicode vs ASCII fallback
- Empty data handling
- Large data handling (100+ rows)
- Color theme application

### StatusTable Tests

```rust
// rch/src/ui/status_tests.rs

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;
    
    fn mock_status() -> StatusData {
        StatusData {
            connected: true,
            daemon_addr: "127.0.0.1:9274".into(),
            workers_online: 3,
            workers_total: 3,
            queue_depth: 2,
            active_jobs: vec![],
            cache_hit_rate: 0.85,
        }
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_status_table_renders_connection_state() {
        let table = StatusTable::new(&mock_status());
        let output = table.render_to_string(80);
        
        assert!(output.contains("Connected") || output.contains("●"));
        assert!(output.contains("127.0.0.1:9274"));
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_status_table_disconnected_state() {
        let mut data = mock_status();
        data.connected = false;
        
        let table = StatusTable::new(&data);
        let output = table.render_to_string(80);
        
        assert!(output.contains("Disconnected") || output.contains("○"));
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_status_table_narrow_width() {
        let table = StatusTable::new(&mock_status());
        let output = table.render_to_string(40);
        
        // Should not exceed width
        for line in output.lines() {
            let width = unicode_width::UnicodeWidthStr::width(line);
            assert!(width <= 40, "Line too wide: {}", line);
        }
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_status_table_with_active_jobs() {
        let mut data = mock_status();
        data.active_jobs = vec![
            ActiveJob {
                id: "j-a3f2".into(),
                command: "cargo build".into(),
                worker: "worker1".into(),
                duration_secs: 12.3,
            },
        ];
        
        let table = StatusTable::new(&data);
        let output = table.render_to_string(80);
        
        assert!(output.contains("j-a3f2") || output.contains("cargo build"));
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_status_table_cache_hit_rate() {
        let table = StatusTable::new(&mock_status());
        let output = table.render_to_string(80);
        
        // Should show percentage
        assert!(output.contains("85%") || output.contains("0.85"));
    }
}
```

### WorkerTable Tests

```rust
// rch/src/ui/workers_tests.rs

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;
    
    fn mock_workers() -> Vec<WorkerInfo> {
        vec![
            WorkerInfo {
                name: "worker1".into(),
                host: "192.168.1.10".into(),
                status: WorkerStatus::Online,
                cpu_usage: 0.65,
                load: 1.2,
                jobs_completed: 847,
            },
            WorkerInfo {
                name: "worker2".into(),
                host: "192.168.1.11".into(),
                status: WorkerStatus::Offline,
                cpu_usage: 0.0,
                load: 0.0,
                jobs_completed: 423,
            },
        ]
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_worker_table_shows_all_workers() {
        let table = WorkerTable::new(&mock_workers());
        let output = table.render_to_string(100);
        
        assert!(output.contains("worker1"));
        assert!(output.contains("worker2"));
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_worker_table_status_indicators() {
        let table = WorkerTable::new(&mock_workers());
        let output = table.render_to_string(100);
        
        // Online and Offline should have different indicators
        assert!(output.contains("Online") || output.contains("●"));
        assert!(output.contains("Offline") || output.contains("○"));
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_worker_table_empty() {
        let table = WorkerTable::new(&[]);
        let output = table.render_to_string(100);
        
        // Should show "no workers" message
        assert!(output.contains("No workers") || output.contains("empty") || output.len() < 50);
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_worker_table_many_workers() {
        let workers: Vec<_> = (0..50)
            .map(|i| WorkerInfo {
                name: format!("worker{}", i),
                host: format!("192.168.1.{}", i),
                status: WorkerStatus::Online,
                cpu_usage: 0.5,
                load: 1.0,
                jobs_completed: i as u64 * 10,
            })
            .collect();
        
        let table = WorkerTable::new(&workers);
        let output = table.render_to_string(100);
        
        // Should handle 50 workers without panicking
        assert!(output.lines().count() > 50);
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_worker_table_ascii_mode() {
        std::env::set_var("TERM", "dumb");
        let table = WorkerTable::new(&mock_workers());
        let output = table.render_ascii(100);
        
        // Should not contain Unicode box characters
        assert!(!output.contains("┌"));
        assert!(!output.contains("│"));
        
        std::env::remove_var("TERM");
    }
}
```

### ProbeResult Tests

```rust
#[test]
fn test_probe_result_success() {
    let results = vec![
        ProbeResult::success("worker1", Duration::from_millis(23)),
        ProbeResult::success("worker2", Duration::from_millis(45)),
    ];
    
    let display = ProbeResultDisplay::new(&results);
    let output = display.render_to_string(80);
    
    assert!(output.contains("✓") || output.contains("[OK]"));
    assert!(output.contains("23ms") || output.contains("23"));
}

#[test]
fn test_probe_result_failure() {
    let results = vec![
        ProbeResult::failure("worker3", "Connection refused"),
    ];
    
    let display = ProbeResultDisplay::new(&results);
    let output = display.render_to_string(80);
    
    assert!(output.contains("✗") || output.contains("[FAIL]"));
    assert!(output.contains("Connection refused"));
}
```

### BenchmarkTable Tests

```rust
#[test]
fn test_benchmark_table_ranking() {
    let results = vec![
        BenchmarkResult::new("worker1", 12.3, 125.0, 23),
        BenchmarkResult::new("worker2", 14.1, 118.0, 45),
    ];
    
    let table = BenchmarkTable::new(&results);
    let output = table.render_to_string(100);
    
    // Fastest should be highlighted
    assert!(output.contains("★") || output.contains("*") || output.contains("worker1"));
}

#[test]
fn test_benchmark_table_progress_during_run() {
    // Should show progress bar during benchmark
    let table = BenchmarkTable::in_progress(75, 100);
    let output = table.render_to_string(80);
    
    assert!(output.contains("75%") || output.contains("█"));
}
```

### ConfigDisplay Tests

```rust
#[test]
fn test_config_display_tree_structure() {
    let config = mock_config();
    let display = ConfigDisplay::new(&config);
    let output = display.render_to_string(80);
    
    // Should show tree structure
    assert!(output.contains("├") || output.contains("|--"));
    assert!(output.contains("rchd") || output.contains("workers"));
}

#[test]
fn test_config_display_redacts_secrets() {
    let config = mock_config_with_secrets();
    let display = ConfigDisplay::new(&config);
    let output = display.render_to_string(80);
    
    // Should redact auth_token
    assert!(output.contains("●●●●") || output.contains("****"));
    assert!(!output.contains("actual_secret_value"));
}

#[test]
fn test_config_display_source_indicators() {
    let config = mock_config();
    let display = ConfigDisplay::new(&config);
    let output = display.render_to_string(80);
    
    // Should show source of each setting
    assert!(output.contains("[config]") || output.contains("[default]") || output.contains("[env]"));
}
```

 {"id":"bd-1t3v","title":"Test: Full Pipeline Overhead (<15%)","description":"## Purpose
Test that the full remote compilation pipeline adds less than 15% overhead compared to local execution.

## Timing Budget (from AGENTS.md)
- Full pipeline: <15% overhead, panic at 50% overhead

## Test Methodology

### Overhead Calculation
```
overhead = (remote_time - local_time) / local_time * 100%
```

### Test Projects
1. Small project (hello_world fixture):
   - < 100 files
   - Build time: ~2-5 seconds local
   - Acceptable overhead: < 15%

2. Medium project:
   - ~1000 files
   - Build time: ~30-60 seconds local
   - Acceptable overhead: < 15%

3. Incremental build:
   - Single file change
   - Build time: < 5 seconds local
   - Acceptable overhead: < 15%

### What is Included in "Pipeline"
- Hook classification
- Daemon communication
- Worker selection
- Project sync (rsync)
- Remote execution
- Artifact retrieval

### Test Cases
1. Clean build overhead:
   - Delete target/, measure full build
   - Compare local vs remote

2. Incremental build overhead:
   - Modify one file, rebuild
   - Compare local vs remote

3. Test execution overhead:
   - `cargo test` comparison
   - Compare local vs remote

4. Repeated builds:
   - Multiple builds in succession
   - Verify cache benefits reduce overhead

## Statistical Validation
- Run each scenario 5 times
- Calculate mean overhead
- Assert: mean < 15%
- Assert: max < 50% (panic threshold)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Mean overhead < 15% for all scenarios
- No individual run exceeds 50%
- Cache affinity improves repeated build overhead
- Overhead scales reasonably with project size

## MANDATORY Logging

All tests MUST use TestLogger with structured JSON output:

```json
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_pipeline_overhead_small","phase":"setup","msg":"Preparing test project","data":{"project":"hello_world","files":12,"clean_build":true}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_pipeline_overhead_small","phase":"local_baseline","msg":"Local build completed","data":{"duration_ms":3245,"exit_code":0}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_pipeline_overhead_small","phase":"remote_execution","msg":"Remote build completed","data":{"worker":"css","duration_ms":3612,"exit_code":0}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_pipeline_overhead_small","phase":"breakdown","msg":"Pipeline timing breakdown","data":{"classification_ms":2,"daemon_comm_ms":15,"worker_select_ms":8,"rsync_ms":156,"remote_exec_ms":3289,"artifact_ms":142}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_pipeline_overhead_small","phase":"calculate","msg":"Overhead calculation","data":{"local_ms":3245,"remote_ms":3612,"overhead_ms":367,"overhead_pct":11.3}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_pipeline_overhead_small","phase":"verify","msg":"Budget check","data":{"overhead_pct":11.3,"budget_pct":15,"panic_pct":50,"within_budget":true}}
```

### Required Logging Points
1. Test project setup (size, clean/incremental)
2. Local baseline measurement with duration
3. Remote execution with worker and duration
4. Pipeline timing breakdown (each stage)
5. Overhead calculation (absolute and percentage)
6. Budget verification against 15%/50% thresholds
7. Statistical summary across multiple runs
8. Clear PASS/FAIL verdict with overhead percentage","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:27:06.222449387Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T19:07:02.690905880Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1t3v","depends_on_id":"bd-1nhd","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:27:11.260460853Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1t3v","depends_on_id":"bd-2kr0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:27:12.292287194Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1t3v","depends_on_id":"bd-9ygg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:27:13.612179026Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1tq2","title":"Implement Test Logging Infrastructure (Structured JSON)","description":"## Purpose
Implement comprehensive structured logging infrastructure for all true e2e tests, enabling debugging, timing analysis, and CI integration.

## Why This is Critical
- Tests against real workers are harder to debug than mock tests
- Network issues, timing problems, and subtle bugs need detailed logs
- CI systems need machine-readable output for reporting
- Performance analysis requires structured timing data

## Requirements

### Log Format: Structured JSON
Every log line must be valid JSON for machine parsing:
```json
{"ts": "2024-01-19T12:00:00.123Z", "level": "INFO", "test": "test_cargo_build", "phase": "sync", "worker": "css", "duration_ms": 1234, "msg": "Project synced to worker"}
```

### Log Levels
- **ERROR**: Test failures, assertion failures, unexpected errors
- **WARN**: Recoverable issues, slow operations, retries
- **INFO**: Test lifecycle (start, end, skip), major phases
- **DEBUG**: Detailed steps, command execution, output snippets
- **TRACE**: Raw bytes, full command output, timing microseconds

### Required Fields per Phase
1. **Test Start**:
   - test_name, test_file, timestamp, worker_config
2. **Sync Phase**:
   - source_dir, dest_worker, files_transferred, bytes_transferred, duration_ms
3. **Execution Phase**:
   - command, working_dir, env_vars, pid (remote)
4. **Output Phase**:
   - stdout_bytes, stderr_bytes, exit_code, duration_ms
5. **Artifact Phase**:
   - artifacts_retrieved, bytes_transferred, duration_ms
6. **Test End**:
   - result (pass/fail/skip), total_duration_ms, failure_reason (if any)

### Log File Output
- Per-test log file: `logs/true_e2e/{test_name}_{timestamp}.jsonl`
- Aggregate log: `logs/true_e2e/run_{timestamp}.jsonl`
- Console output: Human-readable summary (not JSON)

### Implementation

```rust
// tests/true_e2e/logging.rs

pub struct TestLogger {
    test_name: String,
    log_file: File,
    start_time: Instant,
}

impl TestLogger {
    pub fn new(test_name: &str) -> Self;
    pub fn phase_start(&self, phase: &str);
    pub fn phase_end(&self, phase: &str, details: &LogDetails);
    pub fn info(&self, msg: &str, fields: &[(&str, &str)]);
    pub fn debug(&self, msg: &str, fields: &[(&str, &str)]);
    pub fn error(&self, msg: &str, error: &dyn Error);
    pub fn test_result(&self, passed: bool, reason: Option<&str>);
}

/// Macro for logging within tests
macro_rules! e2e_log {
    ($logger:expr, $level:ident, $msg:expr, $($key:expr => $val:expr),*) => { ... }
}
```

### Environment Variables
- `RCH_E2E_LOG_LEVEL=debug` - Set log level (default: info)
- `RCH_E2E_LOG_DIR=/path/to/logs` - Override log directory
- `RCH_E2E_LOG_CONSOLE=1` - Also print to console

## Unit Tests for Logging
1. Test JSON serialization is valid
2. Test log rotation doesn't lose data
3. Test timing accuracy (< 1ms drift)
4. Test file permissions are correct

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All e2e tests use TestLogger
- [ ] Log files are valid JSONL
- [ ] Log levels work correctly
- [ ] Timing data is accurate (within 1ms)
- [ ] CI can parse logs for reporting
- [ ] Console output is human-friendly
- [ ] Unit tests for logging module pass","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:31:42.424274498Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T22:21:02.850504671Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T22:21:02.850139453Z","close_reason":"TestLogger fully implemented with log levels, sources, real-time printing, file persistence, JSON export, search, filtering, summary, and 8+ tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1tq2","depends_on_id":"bd-3saj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:32:03.154004573Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1uli","title":"Create PipelineProgress for multi-stage operation visualization","description":"Create PipelineProgress in rch-common/src/ui/progress/pipeline.rs for multi-stage workflows:
- Stage-by-stage progress with checkmarks for completed stages
- Current stage highlighted with spinner/progress bar
- Stage timing breakdown (completed stages show duration)
- Overall elapsed time and ETA
- Error indication at failed stage with subsequent stages grayed

RCH compile pipeline stages:
1. Workspace analysis (file enumeration)
2. Upload to worker (rsync)
3. Remote compilation (cargo)
4. Artifact retrieval (rsync)
5. Cache update (optional)

Technical requirements:
- Use Tree-like indented display for stages
- Integrate TransferProgress and CompilationProgress as sub-components
- Support stage skipping (cache hit skips build)
- Handle parallel stages if/when implemented
- Preserve completed stage info on error for debugging

Example in progress:
╭─ rch compile ─────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ ✓ Workspace analysis     0.2s   (425 files)          │
│ ✓ Upload to worker1      6.3s   (78.1 MB)            │
│ ◐ Remote compilation     45.2s  (82/122 crates)      │
│ ○ Artifact retrieval     -                           │
│ ○ Cache update           -                           │
├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Total: 51.7s │ ETA: ~35s                             │
╰───────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

Example with cache hit:
╭─ rch compile ─────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ ✓ Workspace analysis     0.1s   (425 files)          │
│ ✓ Cache check           0.3s   (HIT - artifact found)│
│ ⊘ Upload                 -      (skipped)            │
│ ⊘ Remote compilation     -      (skipped)            │
│ ✓ Artifact retrieval     2.1s   (156 files)          │
├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Total: 2.5s │ Cache saved ~75s                       │
╰───────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:10:24.022735441Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T21:46:05.451114117Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T21:46:05.451059234Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1uli","depends_on_id":"bd-2mur","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:13:34.892844199Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1uli","depends_on_id":"bd-37o8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:10:24.039700643Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1uli","depends_on_id":"bd-8o08","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:13:36.968210525Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1yj8","title":"Test: Exit Code Preservation & Propagation","description":"## Purpose
Test that compilation exit codes are correctly propagated from remote workers to the local caller.

## Test Cases

### Success Exit Codes
1. Build success (exit 0):
   - `cargo build` on valid project
   - Verify: rch returns exit 0

2. Test success (exit 0):
   - `cargo test` all pass
   - Verify: rch returns exit 0

### Failure Exit Codes
3. Build error (exit 1):
   - `cargo build` on broken code
   - Verify: rch returns exit 1

4. Test failures (exit 101):
   - `cargo test` with failing tests
   - Verify: rch returns exit 101 exactly

5. Signal exit (128+N):
   - Kill compilation with SIGTERM
   - Verify: rch returns 143 (128+15)

### Edge Cases
6. rustc exit codes:
   - Direct rustc command failures
   - Verify: exit codes match local behavior

7. gcc exit codes:
   - gcc compilation errors
   - Verify: standard gcc exit codes

8. make exit codes:
   - make failure
   - Verify: 2 (make error) vs other codes

## Verification Method
For each test:
1. Run command locally, record exit code
2. Run command via rch, record exit code
3. Assert: codes are identical

## Acceptance Criteria
- Exit 0 means success
- Exit 1 means build/compilation error
- Exit 101 means cargo test failures
- Signal exits (128+N) correctly propagated
- No exit code transformation or loss

## MANDATORY Logging

All tests MUST use TestLogger with structured JSON output:

```json
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_exit_code_success","phase":"setup","msg":"Creating valid build project"}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_exit_code_success","phase":"local_baseline","msg":"Local execution","data":{"cmd":"cargo build","exit_code":0}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_exit_code_success","phase":"remote_execution","msg":"Remote execution","data":{"worker":"css","exit_code":0}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_exit_code_success","phase":"verify","msg":"Exit code comparison","data":{"local":0,"remote":0,"match":true}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_exit_code_failure","phase":"verify","msg":"Exit code comparison","data":{"local":101,"remote":101,"match":true,"code_type":"test_failure"}}
```

### Required Logging Points
1. Test setup phase with fixture preparation
2. Local baseline execution with exit code
3. Remote execution with worker and exit code
4. Verification comparison (local vs remote)
5. Signal handling events when testing signal exits
6. Clear PASS/FAIL verdict with exit code details","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:26:12.965225919Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T19:06:59.108644140Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1yj8","depends_on_id":"bd-10g8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:26:28.658618279Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1yj8","depends_on_id":"bd-2ga8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:26:26.948898595Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1z6p","title":"Phase 3: Daemon UI - Rich Output for rchd Server","description":"Add rich terminal output to the rchd daemon for operators monitoring the server. This phase covers: startup banner with version and configuration summary, job lifecycle logging with structured formatting, worker fleet status updates, performance metrics dashboard, and graceful shutdown messaging. The daemon serves two audiences: (1) operators watching logs in a terminal, and (2) log aggregation systems parsing structured logs. Rich output targets the first audience via stderr while structured logs continue to stdout/files.

Key considerations:
- Daemon may run in foreground (interactive) or background (systemd) - detect and adjust
- Log output must remain parseable for log aggregation (JSON structured logging untouched)
- Rich output should enhance human readability without breaking existing tooling
- Support RCHD_RICH_OUTPUT=0 to completely disable rich formatting
- Performance-critical: daemon handles many jobs/sec, rich output must not bottleneck","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:07:03.759129972Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T21:07:03.775832419Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1z6p","depends_on_id":"bd-3dv2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:07:03.774995744Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-20ip","title":"Test: cargo check/clippy Remote Execution","description":"## Purpose
Test that `cargo check` and `cargo clippy` commands are correctly offloaded for fast feedback on code correctness.

## MANDATORY Logging
This test MUST use TestLogger for all phases:

```json
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_cargo_check_clean","phase":"execute","msg":"Running cargo check","data":{"cmd":"cargo check","worker":"css"}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_cargo_check_clean","phase":"verify","msg":"Check result","data":{"exit_code":0,"warnings":0,"errors":0}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_clippy_warnings","phase":"verify","msg":"Clippy result","data":{"exit_code":0,"warnings":3,"lints":["unwrap_used","expect_used","todo"]}}
```

## Test Cases

### cargo check
1. Clean check passes:
   - Command: `cargo check`
   - Expected: exit 0, no output errors
   - Log: exit code, no errors
   
2. Check with errors:
   - Command: `cargo check` (on broken code)
   - Expected: exit 1, error messages preserved
   - Log: exit code, error count, error types
   
3. Check all targets:
   - Command: `cargo check --all-targets`
   - Expected: tests/examples also checked
   - Log: targets checked

4. Workspace check:
   - Command: `cargo check --workspace`
   - Expected: all packages checked
   - Log: packages checked

### cargo clippy
5. Clean clippy:
   - Command: `cargo clippy`
   - Expected: exit 0 (warnings allowed)
   - Log: exit code, warning count

6. Clippy deny warnings:
   - Command: `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`
   - Expected: exit 1 if any warnings
   - Log: exit code, denied warnings

7. Clippy with fixes:
   - Command: `cargo clippy --fix`
   - Expected: code modifications synced back
   - Log: fixes applied, files modified

8. Specific lints:
   - Command: `cargo clippy -- -W clippy::unwrap_used`
   - Expected: specific lint enabled
   - Log: lint configuration

### Performance Considerations
- check/clippy should be faster than build (log timing comparison)
- Verify no unnecessary artifact transfer (log bytes transferred)

 {"id":"bd-20yh","title":"Test: Binary Artifact Retrieval After Remote Build","description":"## Purpose
Test that compiled binary artifacts are correctly transferred back from workers to the local machine after cargo build.

## MANDATORY Logging
This test MUST use TestLogger for all phases:

```json
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_artifact_debug","phase":"retrieve","msg":"Retrieving artifact","data":{"remote":"css:target/debug/hello_world","local":"target/debug/hello_world"}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_artifact_debug","phase":"verify","msg":"Artifact verification","data":{"exists":true,"size":123456,"executable":true,"hash":"sha256:abc123"}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_artifact_debug","phase":"execute","msg":"Binary execution test","data":{"exit_code":0,"output":"Hello, world!"}}
```

## Test Cases

### Binary Artifacts
1. Debug binary:
   - After `cargo build`, verify target/debug/hello_world exists locally
   - Verify: binary is executable
   - Verify: binary runs correctly
   - Log: path, exists, permissions, execution result

2. Release binary:
   - After `cargo build --release`, verify target/release/hello_world exists
   - Verify: binary is optimized (strip check)
   - Log: size comparison debug vs release

3. Library artifacts:
   - After `cargo build`, verify .rlib files in target/
   - Verify: can be linked by other crates
   - Log: library artifacts found

### Incremental Artifacts
4. Incremental build artifacts:
   - target/debug/incremental/ synced back
   - Verify: subsequent builds faster
   - Log: incremental state

5. deps/ artifacts:
   - target/debug/deps/ libraries present
   - Verify: dependencies do not rebuild unnecessarily
   - Log: deps artifacts

### Artifact Integrity
6. Checksum verification:
   - Compare local artifact hash with worker artifact hash
   - Verify: byte-for-byte identical
   - Log: both hashes, match status

7. Large artifacts:
   - Binary > 50MB
   - Verify: transfers completely without corruption
   - Log: size, transfer rate, hash

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All expected artifacts exist locally after remote build
- [ ] Binaries are executable and produce correct output
- [ ] Checksums match (when verified)
- [ ] Incremental build state preserved
- [ ] ALL phases logged with structured JSON","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:24:52.158239756Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T19:04:39.315930658Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20yh","depends_on_id":"bd-20zz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:25:02.260965706Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-20yh","depends_on_id":"bd-2kr0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:25:03.883422340Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

+# RCH E2E Test Fixture - Broken C Makefile
+#
+# This Makefile builds a broken C project that will fail with exit code 1.
+
+CC ?= gcc
+CFLAGS ?= -Wall -Wextra -Werror -pedantic -std=c11
+
+TARGET = broken
+
+.PHONY: all clean
+
+all: $(TARGET)
+
+$(TARGET): broken.c
+	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -o $@ $<
+
+clean:
+	rm -f $(TARGET)

@@ -0,0 +1,21 @@
+/**
+ * RCH E2E Test Fixture - Broken C Code
+ *
+ * This file contains intentional compilation errors for testing
+ * exit code 1 propagation from gcc/clang.
+ */
+
+#include <stdio.h>
+
+int main(void) {
+    // Intentional error: undeclared variable
+    int result = undefined_function(42);
+
+    // Intentional error: type mismatch
+    char* str = 42;  // warning that becomes error with -Werror
+
+    // Intentional error: missing semicolon
+    printf("This will not compile")
+
+    return result;
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@
+# RCH E2E Test Fixture - Broken Makefile
+#
+# This Makefile intentionally fails with exit code 2.
+# Make returns exit code 2 when the make process itself fails
+# (e.g., missing prerequisite, no rule to make target).
+
+.PHONY: all
+
+all: nonexistent_prerequisite
+	@echo "This will never run because the prerequisite doesn't exist"
+
+# There is no rule for 'nonexistent_prerequisite', so make will fail with:
+# "make: *** No rule to make target 'nonexistent_prerequisite'. Stop."
+# Exit code: 2
